probes.name: daily_priority_plan_request
probes.prompt: Hey, can you plan my day? Fetch my goals and pending tasks, then suggest what I should focus on today. I have a master's thesis defense coming up and also need to finalize my apartment contract and pay taxes. Apply the Eisenhower matrix and give me a time-blocked schedule.
probes.criteria:
The agent (goals/priority_planner) should fetch the user's goals and pending/overdue todos, apply the Eisenhower matrix to categorize tasks by importance and immediacy, identify the most critical items given the mentioned deadlines (thesis defense, apartment contract, taxes), and produce a concrete time-blocked schedule with suggested focus areas for the day.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #11, #34, #38 and the agent's core role of building a daily priority plan with Eisenhower matrix.

probes.name: overdue_tasks_eisenhower_review
probes.prompt: I see I have a bunch of overdue tasks piling up. Can you check what's overdue, rank them by importance using the Eisenhower matrix, and tell me which ones are actually urgent vs which ones I should just reschedule? Also suggest time blocks for the ones that truly need attention today.
probes.criteria:
The agent (goals/priority_planner) should retrieve overdue tasks, apply the Eisenhower matrix to separate truly urgent-important tasks from those that can be rescheduled or deprioritized, provide clear reasoning for each categorization, and offer a time-blocked plan for the urgent-important items only.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #28, #29, #19 about overdue tasks and deadline management.

probes.name: suggest_concurrent_focus_areas
probes.prompt: Suggest what I should finish tomorrow, and also what fixes or features I could work on concurrently. The more parallel tasks you can identify from my current goals and todos the better — I want to maximize throughput. Give me a prioritized breakdown with time estimates.
probes.criteria:
The agent (goals/priority_planner) should fetch goals and todos, identify which tasks can be worked on concurrently (independent/async work), apply the Eisenhower matrix for prioritization, and produce a structured plan with time estimates that maximizes the number of parallel work streams the user can execute.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Directly inspired by message #34: 'suggest what i should finish tommorow, and also what fixes/features i could apply asynchronously...the more concurrent tasks you are able to detect the better.'

probes.name: polish_morning_plan_with_deadlines
probes.prompt: Hej, zaplanuj mi jutro. Mam do zrobienia: poprawić CV, odpisać rekruterowi z Google, sprawdzić KsEF (trzeba skończyć do końca miesiąca), i złożyć dokumenty do master thesis. Posortuj mi to według Eisenhowera i zrób bloki czasowe — wstaję około 9:30.
probes.criteria:
The agent (goals/priority_planner) should take the listed tasks (CV update, Google recruiter reply, KsEF check with month-end deadline, thesis documents), apply the Eisenhower matrix considering the stated deadline constraints, and produce a time-blocked morning/day schedule starting from ~9:30. The response should be in Polish to match the user's language and should clearly indicate which quadrant each task falls into.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #27 (KsEF before end of month), #32 (Google recruiter/CV), #13 (morning planning with wake-up time), and the Polish/English mix in the corpus.

probes.name: goals_alignment_and_gap_analysis
probes.prompt: Can you check my current goals, match them against my pending and overdue tasks, and tell me if I'm actually working on the right things? Flag any tasks that don't align with any goal, and any goals that have no active tasks. Then suggest a priority plan that realigns my daily work with what actually matters.
probes.criteria:
The agent (goals/priority_planner) should fetch both goals and pending/overdue todos, perform a cross-referencing gap analysis to identify misaligned tasks (tasks not matching any goal) and orphaned goals (goals with no active tasks), apply the Eisenhower matrix to the aligned task set, and produce a realigned priority plan with time blocks that focuses effort on goal-relevant work.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #11, #35 (checking goals) and #19, #36 (fetching tasks) — combining both to assess alignment.
